Scenario.title: Outer Space Phishing Campaign Targets Healthcare Data

overview.description: The Outer Space campaign leverages a phishing attack to infiltrate a healthcare organization, deploy malware, and disrupt operations through data deletion.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 164 Name: Mobile Phishing Description: An adversary targets mobile phone users with a phishing attack for the purpose of soliciting account passwords or sensitive information from the user.
stage.description: The Outer Space campaign initiates with a phishing email containing malicious links or attachments. Employees receive emails impersonating trusted entities, such as healthcare providers or vendors, urging them to click on links or open files. This phishing attempt exploits human error and weak authentication mechanisms to gain initial access to the network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.technique_name: Malware Deployment via Remote Access Tool
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 445 Name: Malicious Logic Insertion into Product Software via Configuration Management Manipulation Description: An adversary exploits a configuration management system so that malicious logic is inserted into a software product's build, update, or deployment environment.
stage.description: Once initial access is achieved, the remote access tool (RAT) deploys malware onto the infected device. This malware establishes persistence and enables further lateral movement within the network. The RAT operates undetected, leveraging known vulnerabilities in outdated software and insufficient patch management practices.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 546 Name: Incomplete Data Deletion in a Multi-Tenant Environment Description: An adversary obtains unauthorized information due to insecure or incomplete data deletion in a multi-tenant environment.
stage.description: The final phase involves the deletion of critical healthcare data, including patient records and operational databases. This action disrupts normal business operations, causing significant financial loss and reputational damage. The deletion appears random, but it systematically targets essential systems, maximizing the impact of the attack.

Scenario.summary: The Outer Space campaign demonstrates a basic yet effective attack strategy, using phishing as the entry point, followed by malware deployment, and culminating in destructive data deletion. The simplicity of the tactics makes it accessible to novice attackers while still achieving significant disruption in the healthcare sector.
